point_reviews.reason: The document states COVID-19 is caused by SARS-CoV-2, while the common cold and flu are caused by different viruses.
The document mentions that COVID-19 symptoms can range from mild to severe, similar to the flu and common cold.
The document indicates that COVID-19 can lead to serious complications, including difficulty breathing and multisystem inflammatory syndrome.
The document confirms that loss of taste or smell is a symptom of COVID-19, which is not common in the flu or common cold.
The document states that COVID-19 has a longer incubation period of 2 to 14 days compared to the common cold and flu.
The document mentions that older adults and those with certain medical conditions are at higher risk for severe COVID-19 illness.
The document states that COVID-19 can cause serious medical complications and lead to death, unlike the common cold or flu.
The external evidence confirms that loss of taste or smell is a symptom of COVID-19, which is not typical for the common cold or flu.
The document confirms that COVID-19 is caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus, while the common cold and flu are caused by different viruses.
The document mentions that COVID-19 has a longer incubation period of 2 to 14 days compared to the common cold and flu.
The document does not explicitly state that older adults and those with certain medical conditions are at higher risk for severe COVID-19 illness compared to the flu.
